message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker statuses for completed and in-progress work

Sync issue tracker to reflect implementation progress:
- bd-2n0.4 (trace/replay/snapshot): open -> closed (implemented in trace.c)
- bd-hwb.9 (safety posture checks): in_progress -> closed
- bd-2cw.3 (cancellation): open -> in_progress (implementation landed)
- bd-hwb.15 (fault containment policy): open -> in_progress (enum + API declared)
- bd-1md.2 (conformance runner): open -> in_progress (fixture runner added to CI)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Robustness e2e scripts must stress allocator/resource boundaries, malformed-input edges, and injected fault paths with deterministic outcomes.

- Implement scripts for exhaustion boundaries, endian/unaligned safety checks, stale-handle misuse, and controlled fault-injection modes.
- Capture deterministic outcomes, failure-atomicity evidence, and containment-path behavior in structured manifests.
- Emit minimized first-failure summaries with precise rerun hints and artifact pointers.
- Link robustness e2e lanes to corresponding unit/invariant obligations and forbidden-behavior rows.

- Robustness e2e suite reproduces deterministic outcomes across reruns and environments.

Plan section 7.3.4 introduces a public API misuse catalog to compensate for missing Rust compile-time misuse rejection.

 {"id":"bd-1md.20","title":"Implement shared e2e harness contract for scenario packs, manifests, and rerun UX","description":"Background:
E2E family beads need a shared harness layer so scripts stay consistent, diagnosable, and maintainable across core/robustness/vertical packs.

- Integrate harness with structured log schema and conformance-runner hooks.

- Manifest/log outputs are schema-consistent across script families.

 {"id":"bd-2cw","title":"EPIC: Runtime Kernel, Scheduling, Cancellation, Timer, and Channel Semantics","description":"Background:
Kernel behavior is where semantic fidelity is most fragile: ordering, cancellation, and queueing rules can silently diverge.

Goal:
Implement deterministic kernel loop and channel/timer semantics with rigorous fairness/starvation and correctness checks.

Must include:
- deterministic tie-break and event sequencing
- cancellation propagation checkpoints
- timer wheel semantics and O(1) cancel behavior
- bounded two-phase MPSC semantics

Why this serves users:
- reproducible behavior for incident analysis
- robust performance and correctness on both low-latency and constrained hardware.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.106645851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:20:12.160584077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-5","phase-6","runtime","scheduler"]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.1","title":"Implement arena-backed region/task/obligation tables with generation-safe handles","description":"Background:
Runtime storage for lifecycle entities must be performant and safe under churn, cancellation, and reuse.

Subtasks:
- Implement arena-backed table structures for regions, tasks, and obligations.
- Enforce generation-safe handle validation on every access path.
- Integrate debug quarantine behavior for hardening mode.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Table operations are deterministic and test-covered.
- Handle reuse and stale-handle cases are explicitly validated.
- No direct external mutable references escape API boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.177963236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:14:44.551376753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:14:44.551358800Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-4","runtime","tables"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.177963236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.773876860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ix8","title":"EPIC: Build System, Portability Matrix, and Repository Scaffold","description":"Background:

Establish build/repo infrastructure for GCC/Clang/MSVC plus embedded cross-target matrix, including QEMU validation harness.

Must preserve:
- dependency-free core assumptions
- profile-aware compile paths
- reproducible, diagnosable failures

Why this serves users:
- HFT teams need deterministic reproducible builds
- embedded teams need cross-target confidence before deployment
- maintainers need fast feedback loops with clear failure surfaces.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
